preview_text:
The model favours Portugal to win at 76%, with DR Congo assigned only a 4% chance of victory. Portugal enters as a heavy favourite, though the 20% draw probability reflects meaningful competitive uncertainty in a knockout context.

The gap between Portugal's win probability and DR Congo's creates a sharply asymmetric match forecast. A draw remains plausible at one-in-five odds, but a DR Congo upset sits well outside the model's central estimate. Portugal is projected as the substantially stronger side, though not to a degree that eliminates the underdog's path to advancement.

The model and sportsbook consensus are closely aligned on Portugal's win probability, separated by under 2 percentage points. The books assign DR Congo nearly 10%, roughly double the model's 4%, and trim the draw to 17%—suggesting the market weights upset scenarios more heavily than the statistical forecast. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these views. No Polymarket contract has been posted yet.
